text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Population:

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Actually exists.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: We want to know

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: about it!

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Sample of

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: We can't look at the entire population so we

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: get a small piece of it, hopefully the same

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: shape.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Distributions are defined by parameters.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: The normal distribution is defined by a mean

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: and standard deviation (possible movement of X around )

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: We don't know what the population distribution looks like.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: We don't even know if its normally distributed.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: s

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: s

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: is a random variable.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Every time we ask "what is the value of X ?", we can get a

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: different answer. These answers are distributed in some way.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: That is many of the answers could be clumped around a certain value.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Or many different appear equally likely (uniformly distributed).

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: A distribution

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Also a

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: distribution

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: What can we answer with the sample mean ?

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: By itself, not a whole lot but the CENTRAL LIMIT THEOREM (CLT) tells us that the

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: distribution of s will be normally distributed centered around

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Yet another

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: distribution.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: The random

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: variable is the

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: sample means of

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: x.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: The Central Limit

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Theorem is a rabbit hole.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: There is the proof for the CLT,

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: different versions of CLTs under

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: different conditions (e.g., when each answer to a question

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: about x is dependent on the previous answer) , etc.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Cool, but let's just take it for granted

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: for now.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: A sample of Is a collection of answers to the question "what is the value of X?"

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: We try to estimate things about from this collection of answers.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: It's a mini version of the population (assuming we did a good job).

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: We want to "assume" things about from

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: INFERENTIAL STATISTICS

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: The standard deviation (back to distance)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: The variance : The average (squared) distance of

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: each element in

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: from the sample mean (in distance squared units)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: The mean of the collection

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: So the should behave in accordance with this principle. We cannot directly

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: answer questions about but maybe we can answer questions about this distribution

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Let's call it .

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: From SAMPLE STATISTICS to ESTIMATORS

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: There should be a distinction made between SAMPLE STATISTICS and an ESTIMATOR. The former just describes the sample, . The latter is a "device" that we build

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: to ESTIMATE something about . What we want is a GOOD ESTIMATOR. A GOOD estimator is one whose outputs are:

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. Unbiased : It does not tend to under or over estimate.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2. Close to the target : It's not enough to not be biased, it has to actually provide a result that is close to the actual value. (efficient or low variance)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: It can be unbiased by oscillating wildly between being far away from a target number in either direction. This is an unbiased but bad estimator.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: The general principle for

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: making inference about any

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: population feature

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: (e.g., the mean, maximum, etc.)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: is to first determine if that

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: feature conforms to any of

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: the applicable central limit

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: theorems. This is because

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: depending on the how the

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: feature is calculated (e.g., is it

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: additive like the mean or

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: not like the maximum), the

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: sampling distribution for it

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: will be different.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: THEOREM

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Recall, what we ultimately want is a full picture of the distribution of . For a given distribution, we can ask some basic questions :

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. What is the value I should expect of each example in ?

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2. How much can I expect an example to vary around this average value ?

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 3. Can take some very large or small values that gives me a skewed picture of where the expected value is ?

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Each additional question gives a higher resolution image of the distribution, like adding layers to a cake. The base layer is the expected value, and each additional layer of

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: frosting (eg., variance) adds more detail to the cake . These layers are described in the language of MOMENTS.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: In general:

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: where is the reference, and is the moment (i.e., layer of the cake we want to add), each centered around the first the central moment (Moment 1).

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. Moment 1 : The center (Location)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. : The expected value of which for a random variable is the mean.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2. : sample mean.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2. Moment 2 : The spread (width)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. = population Variance.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2. = sample variance

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 3. Moment 3 : Skewness (or asymmetry)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. = Population asymmetry.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2. = sample asymmetry.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 4. etc..

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Expanding out , with each increasing level , we can get the full distribution. Now , if we wanted to, we could stop here and call it a day. The data is what we

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: have, we get sample statistics from it and we are satisfied in the description.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: But, we know we just got a sample of the full distribution. How can we judge if the sample we happened to get is any good ?

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: When we start to make claims about the population from , we are in INFERENCE land. This means we have to provide some evidence that our sample is good enough

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: to support our claims.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. Building an estimator of .

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Turns out that the sample mean is already an unbiased estimator of

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: is an unbiased estimator of , IN GENERAL . That is, the sample mean is an unbiased estimator of the first moment of . There is a simple proof for this, based on the

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: properties of the expected value of a random variable. However, how GOOD of an estimator ANY SPECIFIC SAMPLE MEAN is depends on the characteristics of our sample.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Suppose that has a fixed variance. Suppose we build 2 estimators and with sample size and

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: A distribution

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Every time you pull a value of out of the population, their is a chance you

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: pull a value far out into the tails of the distribution, . It will be small

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: compared to the chance of picking one around but its IMPACT on

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: the outcome we care about, an estimate of , will be huge. Increasing

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: the sample size simply gives you more chances to cancel out the impact

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: of these tail-end draws. You pick one from the right end, you pick one

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: from the left end, etc and it cancels out. Over all, you will pick more and

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: more around which improves the estimate.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Thus a good estimator has low bias AND low variance. That is, it does not systematically

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: return a lower/higher value than the target and it doesn't bounce around a lot. That is,

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: the expected value of the estimator is close to target AND has low variance. The variance

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: of the estimator is the variance of the sampling distribution.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Sampling distribution of

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Sampling distribution of

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. Assessing the variance of the estimator .

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Recall how we defined the estimator:

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: We need to switch lenses a bit. Now the random variable that we care about is and its the variance of this random variable that we are after.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Turns out, this random variable inherits the randomness of its elements (namely, each sample in ).

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. We divided each element in by the sample size.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2. We invoke the additivity of the variance operator for independent random samples : The variance of the sum is the sum of the individual variances:

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: THEOREM

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: THEOREM

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: THEOREM

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 3. Derive the variance of the estimator as :

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 4. Finally, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution , i.e., the STANDARD ERROR.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: This tells us some amazing things.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. With the SE in hand, we can assess how likely it is that we get any . Suppose that we get an +2.5 SE away.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: n

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2. The "goodness" (or more precisely, the certainty) of our estimator goes up

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: as the square root of our sample size. That is, the first few samples gives us

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: a lot of information and each additional sample tells us less, and less.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Low SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: High SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Probability density

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Probability density

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Probability density

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Probability density

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Probability density

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Probability density

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: First, recall that thanks to the CLT , we have some guarantees about . Namely, that it will be normally distributed around the true population mean. Thus, of all the that

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: we could possibly collect, 95% of them will fall within 2 SE of the population mean.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: In both of these cases, the same output of the estimator says two different stories. Relative to the low SE distribution, extreme values of are more likely in the high SE one.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: If the question is "How close to the true population mean is our estimate ? ", for both of these cases, they are the same distance away but we have lower uncertainty on

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: the left than on the right. Given the option of the two, we should choose the one on the left.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: For whatever question we want to ask of our estimator, for example "How likely is that we see this output purely as a consequence of an unlucky draw" ?, we should always

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: prefer the distribution with lower SE. This is because no matter what the answer is, this is the one that lowers our uncertainty the most.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2SE

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: The density is spread out. Lower SE concentrates

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: the density around a particular point. This is a

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: consequence second axiom of probability. The sum of

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: probability of each out come should sum to one :

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: of any of the possible things, one of them HAS to happen.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Take any two neighboring . The difference in probability

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: density (i.e., the chance of seeing a given outcome) between

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: them increases much faster in the low SE case than in the

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: high SE one.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Spreading the total probability over more of the

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: possible outcomes is the definition of increasing

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: uncertainty.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: THEOREM

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Unlike the sample mean, the sample variance is NOT an

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: unbiased estimator of , it is simply the sample variance.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: To get an estimator out of it, we apply a correction.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: THEOREM
